findings.evidence: `rg -n "set g : ℝ → X := fun t => if 0 ≤ t then f t else x|integral_hasDerivAt_of_tendsto_ae_right|tendsto_average_(orbit_zero|resolvent_integrand)" code/TauCeti/Analysis/Semigroups` hits `Generator.lean:162,169,198` and `Resolvent.lean:206,213,260`.
findings.fix: Introduce a reusable private lemma for right-continuous/continuous-on-`Ici` functions proving `(1/t) • ∫_(0,t] f → f 0`, then specialize it to the orbit and the exponentially weighted orbit.
findings.issue: The Cesaro-average proof is duplicated in `tendsto_average_orbit_zero` and `tendsto_average_resolvent_integrand`, including the same piecewise extension and `integral_hasDerivAt_of_tendsto_ae_right` scaffold.

summary: The remaining proof-quality issue is duplicated substantial FTC/Cesaro-average reasoning in the generator and resolvent proofs. Factor the shared argument into a reusable helper instead of maintaining two near-parallel proof scripts.
